description.abstract: Test that generics with types separated by semicolons are parsed correctly.
description.detailed: See https://docwiki.embarcadero.com/RADStudio/Sydney/en/Constraints_in_Generics: """When you specify constraints, you separate multiple type parameters by semicolons, as you do with a parameter list declaration: """

type.name: TMethodFunc
type.declaration: TMethodFunc = function(Arg: A): R of object;

description.abstract: Generic type aliases with semicolons (in type section)

type.name: TMethodFunc2
type.declaration: TMethodFunc2 = function(Arg1: A; Arg2: B): R of object;

routine.name: Call
routine.declaration: function Call<A;R>(const Arg: A): R; overload;

description.abstract: Call with two type params separated by semicolon

routine.name: Call
routine.declaration: function Call<A;B;R>(const Arg1: A; const Arg2: B): R; overload;

description.abstract: Call with three type params separated by semicolons

routine.name: Execute
routine.declaration: procedure Execute<A;B>(const Arg1: A; const Arg2: B); overload;
